property.privacyNote: Published UI intentionally avoids exact address, phone, payment, or private account details.
property.operatingGoal: Healthy, low-effort outdoor systems with good timing and sane decisions.
coverageMath: A 5,000 sq ft lawn product covers about 61% of the full lawn.
Two 5,000 sq ft units are typically needed for a full-property application.

defaultRules: Mow tall and avoid removing more than about one-third of the blade.
Mulch clippings unless they are clumpy, diseased, or smothering the turf.
Water deeply and infrequently when the lawn actually needs it.
Prioritize September and November fertilizer over summer color chasing.
Seed or overseed in fall when possible.

currentSeason.label: Pre-emergent window
currentSeason.summary: Decide on crabgrass preventer timing, water-in conditions, and whether any spring fertility is actually worth it.
nowFocus: Mow if the turf is actively growing and tall enough.
Confirm whether crabgrass pre-emergent has already been applied.
Spot check weeds and thin areas without trying to solve everything now.
nextFocus: Watch soil moisture after stormy periods.
Avoid heavy spring nitrogen unless irrigation and summer follow-through are realistic.
Start thinking ahead to May heat, disease pressure, and irrigation coverage.
buyPrepFocus: Use the 8,225 sq ft lawn area when deciding whether to buy one or two 5,000 sq ft products.
Record spreader settings when any product is applied.
Keep fall seed and fertilizer planning in view, but do not rush large spring seeding.

weatherAwareness.summary: Homepage weather loads a live 3-day Open-Meteo forecast for ZIP 67147, with this seeded forecast retained as a static fallback.

checks.label: Freeze risk
checks.meaning: Delay sprinkler startup, seeding, or tender plant work if hard freezes are likely.

checks.label: Upcoming rain
checks.meaning: Useful for watering in fertilizer/pre-emergent, risky if storms may wash product away.

checks.label: Heat stress
checks.meaning: Raise mowing height, avoid pushing nitrogen, and water only when turf shows need.

checks.label: Wind/application caution
checks.meaning: Avoid herbicide or granular applications when wind or storms make coverage unreliable.

checks.label: Irrigation relevance
checks.meaning: Rain, soil moisture, and startup status should influence watering recommendations.
